reforms.description: Luxembourg plans to increase public spending on R&D to match private R&D funding, aiming to encourage greater investment by firms in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public R&D funding is expected to stimulate private sector investment, enhancing overall innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to increase public spending on R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the government's approach to R&D funding, aiming to align public investment with private sector contributions, which is crucial for enhancing innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Increase public spending on R&D to match private R&D funding and encourage greater investment by firms.

reforms.description: The government aims to better target public innovation investment towards specific projects to enhance productivity growth and economic diversification.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted public investment in innovation is expected to raise productivity and support economic diversification, which can enhance overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to better target public innovation investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how public funds for innovation are allocated, aiming to improve the effectiveness of public investment in driving productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Public innovation investment should better target specific projects, while corporate R&D investment could be better supported through matching public funding.

reforms.description: The government is committing over 4% of GDP annually to public investment, which will support infrastructure, the green transition, and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment is expected to enhance infrastructure and innovation, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is committing over 4% of GDP annually to public investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural commitment to enhance public investment, which is crucial for supporting innovation and the green transition.
reforms.source_quote: Public investment of over 4% of GDP annually will continue to support infrastructure, the green transition, and innovation.

reforms.description: Luxembourg should increase investment in research and development (R&D) to support long-term productivity, particularly targeting laggard firms to stimulate technological diffusion and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D investment is expected to enhance productivity and innovation, leading to higher incomes and greater labor demand.
reforms.status_evidence: Luxembourg should increase investment in research and development (R&D) to support long-term productivity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at reversing the trend of declining R&D investment, which is crucial for enhancing the country's innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Investment spending, in particular on R&D, could be increased to support more innovation.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's government plans to switch to better-targeted funding of innovation projects in partnership with the private sector, aiming to optimize the outcomes of its R&D funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted funding can enhance firms' absorptive capacity and stimulate private R&D investment, leading to increased innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to switch to better-targeted funding of innovation projects.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how R&D funding is allocated, focusing on partnerships with the private sector to enhance innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: Switching to better targeted funding of innovation projects in partnership with the private sector could optimise the outcomes of its funding.

reforms.description: Luxembourg's Digital Innovation Hub has been implemented to support the digital transformation of the industry, particularly for SMEs, by providing various services including investment matching and training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating digital transformation, the hub aims to enhance productivity and innovation among SMEs, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Luxembourg has recently implemented a Digital Innovation Hub.
reforms.importance_rationale: The hub represents a significant initiative to support SMEs in their digital transformation, which is crucial for enhancing their competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Luxembourg has also recently implemented a Digital Innovation Hub, with a mission to be a ‘one-stop-shop’ for addressing the digital transformation of the Luxembourg industry.

reforms.description: Luxembourg has purchased a super-computer, MeluXina, to enhance its research capabilities in both industrial and academic sectors, promoting the adoption of cutting-edge technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in advanced research infrastructure is expected to boost research output and innovation, thereby enhancing productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The super-computer has been purchased and is set to improve research performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: The acquisition of the super-computer represents a significant investment in research infrastructure that can enhance Luxembourg's research capabilities and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: A super-computer, MeluXina, has been purchased by the authorities and will be used to improve Luxembourg’s performance in research, both industrial and academic.

reforms.description: Luxembourg has increased its green R&D support significantly between 2010 and 2020, enhancing subsidies for energy efficiency investments for both households and firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for green R&D is expected to drive innovation in energy efficiency, contributing to economic growth and sustainability.
reforms.status_evidence: Green R&D support rose sharply between 2010 and 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing green innovation, which is crucial for achieving decarbonisation goals and fostering sustainable economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Green R&D support rising sharply between 2010 and 2020 has been augmented by increases in the subsidies to support energy efficiency investments for both households and firms.

reforms.description: The government introduced a carbon price that applies to all sectors outside the Emissions Trading Scheme, raising the carbon tax rate to EUR 25 in 2022 and planning to increase it to EUR 30 in 2023. This reform aims to provide a clearer price signal to encourage investments in the green transition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the carbon price, the reform incentivizes firms to invest in cleaner technologies and reduces emissions, supporting the transition to a greener economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The carbon tax rate was raised as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in environmental policy, aiming to align Luxembourg's carbon pricing with broader climate objectives and enhance the effectiveness of existing innovation programs.
reforms.source_quote: The government recently introduced a carbon price that applies to all sectors other than those in the ETS.

reforms.description: Luxembourg introduced free public transport across all modes of transport in the country, expanding eligibility to include youth, young students, and low-income households. This initiative aims to improve access to public transport and reduce car usage.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing public transport usage, the initiative aims to reduce reliance on cars, thereby lowering emissions and improving urban mobility.
reforms.status_evidence: Public transport was made free following significant efforts to increase availability and quality.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public transport policy, aiming to fundamentally change commuting patterns and reduce environmental impact.
reforms.source_quote: As of March 2020, Luxembourg introduced free public transport across all modes of transport in the country, expanding eligibility from the youth, young students and the lowest income households.
